dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: What happened to you, one of your incantations backfire or something? Last time I remember you being this small it was potentially all just a dream of mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This is the work of the Enchantress Circe, and I'll have you know, it is entirely intentional! You won't be ridiculing me when you see how elusive I've become.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I didn't mean to ridicule you to begin with, although I think the Shades around here would probably enjoy a bit of verbal hostility before the main event. Come on then, Little Sister!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Sister, you... erm... look, you're causing me to feel a little insecure about my height. How did you get to such a stature, or more importantly, how can I?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, you'd have to pay a visit to the Enchantress Circe over on her remote isle, so it might be far out of your way. The effect is only temporary, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Even your voice is changed! Well, at least it's good to know I'll only feel humiliated here for the time being. Let's see what you've got, Little Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: So where exactly are these Crossroads where you're from? I feel as though, as Security Specialist, I ought to have been fully disclosed by this point.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well the Crossroads are in Erebus, but simply knowing that won't help with locating the place. Have Mother and Father not informed you?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: No. They're probably concerned that if I ever went, I'd want to stay, if you like it so much. That, or they're trying to protect your secrecy. Oh well! We can see each other here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Not yet accustomed to the weight of Gigaros... I used to have a much wider variety of Arms at my disposal, you know! But now it seems they've been picked through.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm quite familiar with a couple of their current bearers, at least. The Blade of the Underworld is in the capable hands of Nemesis, while the Rail of Adamant is in the... hands of Eris.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I happen to miss those! But I was using their Aspects for a while, so perhaps it's all as it should be. They better be well taken care of, better than this old spear.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How did you get Gigaros anyhow? You never told me. Father truly just handed it over as a gift?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I persuaded him like you suggested! Father, if I'm to command respect in my role as Security Specialist, I ought to wield the authority of the House of Hades! He had a lot of bad memories attached to this thing, besides.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: From fighting against you...? While I was growing up Headmistress Hecate told me all about how you did that.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Now she is quite the cagey one, must have served Mother in secrecy way back when. Well if she taught you all your magick and whatnot, then she must know plenty!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: The Princess of the Dead again confronts her willful brother Zagreus, who has unlearned some of his finer fighting-skills whilst trapped in the clutches of the Titan of Time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Hey it's you, old man! I was beginning to wonder where you wound up. Abandoned me for my dear sister, is that it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait, what? Homer's been with me all this time. How do you know each other? What did I miss...?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I can't even begin trying to answer that right now. Homer, you say? I should have figured he'd have such a silly name. Well let's give him another tale to tell!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How are the Houseservants recovering, everybody back on their feet by now? I have fond memories of them... glimpses all through my childhood and growing up.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: They have similarly kind things to say, whenever they're not bewildered by what happened. But yes, everybody's back on their feet, thanks... besides Dusa who doesn't have any.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad to hear it, besides the bewilderment part. You're not officially supposed to speak of what happened, are you? But please tell Dusa and the rest I said hello.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: You got it. And officially or not, you're getting quite the terrifying reputation! Our rumor-spreading court musician Orpheus especially enjoys the tales of dark Melinoë. I take it you're about to show off your daemonic prowess here!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos recently returned to us! You've no idea how grateful he was to be back. Practically jumped right into my arms, although that might have been from abject fear.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm uncertain whether he realized the House of Hades was out of commission for most of the time he was asleep. He left before I could explain. Do you know if he remembers the other past we saw...?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I think so? He's still a bit frazzled from everything... perhaps keyed up from having overslept a bit. He still loves when I return to the House in failure, keeping track of all my deaths. So if you'd like to do him another favor, here's your shot!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos recently returned to us! You've no idea how grateful he was to be back. Practically jumped right into my arms, although that might have been from abject fear.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He left in a bit of a rush, yes. I've seldom met someone so dedicated to their work. Has he been managing all right now that he's back?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I think so? He's still a bit frazzled from everything... perhaps keyed up from having overslept a bit. He still loves when I return to the House in failure, keeping track of all my deaths. So if you'd like to do him another favor, here's your shot!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I know that gleam... you brought my Calling Card along! So has Olympus still been as forthcoming with Boons and such now that things have cooled off a bit?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I think our relatives are quite eager to ensure things remain as-is. Which seems a little strange since it's all still quite a mess... though perhaps not to the previous extent.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Having things remain familiar for a while sounds like quite a pleasant change of pace, now that you mention it. Hey, these vicious battles can be part of our routine!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How have Mother and Father been, and Nyx? All this must be quite an adjustment for them.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Oh they're all right. You're seeing Mother and Father not too infrequently yourself, as far as I've gathered. They're keeping an eye on things in Tartarus, probably in part to see you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Or have some time alone I suppose. Meanwhile your current charge is here in Elysium, entertaining all these Shades?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I'd like to think of it as inspiring moreso than entertaining, but if you must ask so pointedly, then... yes. Keeping the Shades of the Underworld happy is our highest priority! Next to keeping them from getting out, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: How is it in those Crossroads, anyway? Do you have friends, more-than-friends, that sort of thing? Do tell.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I suppose so, yes. Some of each. It's peaceful there. And I can see the Moon and stars under a canopy of trees... it even rains every so often.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Mm, getting rained on. Perhaps at some point I can brave the unpleasant weather and come visit, meet your friends or more-than-friends and all. We have eternity! Unless we somehow manage to kill each other here first.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: You know I'm starting to worry all these Shades here think we truly get along, and that you're not the terrifying harbinger of the Underworld's vengeance you're cracked up to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What if I glower at you like this, and demand your head, if not your soul...?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: That works, and we can always keep our cordial little conversations nice and brief. Now face me, daemon-sister!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So you're back to being Security Specialist! It must be easier trying to break out of the Underworld when the gates are still flung open.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: As a matter of fact, it is, yes! Particularly that very last part where I previously had to get past Cerberus and worse. For now it's more about surveying than escape.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You call this surveying? We're in a battle nearly to the death, all for the entertainment of these bloodthirsty Shades.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Now, now, Sister, the Shades of Elysium are some of our most valuable and influential residents! If spilling a little blood will satisfy them, that seems like a worthy sacrifice.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Fair warning, Sister, I am feeling very spry right now. You must have done something to the Mirror of Night. It's always made me feel more capable, but this is something else.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It isn't from the Mirror, I don't think, but I can safely say it is Night-related. You make a fearsome adversary, Zagreus... imbued with the dark strength of Nyx herself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you did this somehow. Well fine! One of these days or nights I ought to fire up Father's old Pact of Punishment, and make you immensely strong, and we'll see how you like it!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Didn't you mention that a certain Schelemeus was residing with you, wherever your secret witch encampment is? How's he doing?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's as resolute as ever, teaching our loyal Shades our ways. He also serves as a practice partner of sorts. Though, I realize I'm not supposed to speak of him...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Uh-huh. He probably just stands indulging in his twisted desire to be savaged by a variety of powerful weapons. Which I... now realize has started to rub off on me...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There's this one Shade I found in the Mourning Fields who I believe resided in Elysium before. Sounds like you may have met.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Certainly possible, I've run into many Shades, Elysian and not. Can you be more specific? Name, disposition, attire?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Name, unknown; disposition, rather encouraging; attire, entirely in red. From what I gathered, this one used to watch your many battles here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Wait I think I know who you mean! You're talking about my biggest fan. Tell my good Shade I said cheers, will you?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: My biggest fan is in the audience again! Cheers for mentioning I'm back to doing what I do best... defending the title of Champion of Elysium all for the sake of show.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What makes you think that one particular Shade is your biggest fan? Perhaps it's me and I'm not letting on.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Come on, would my biggest fan attempt to slaughter me before these droves of onlookers? Only a fearsome daemon born of nightmare would do such an unconscionable thing!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: So you can just... go to the surface, and head all the way up to Olympus, and not die? I could never get very far.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I owe it to a complicated incantation called Unraveling a Fateful Bond. Without that, I could not get very far either. You must have longed to see Olympus for yourself... I wonder if...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Hey, no need to defy the Fates on my account, Sister! I'd rather leave things be. Perhaps once the Underworld's back in business, I'll feel a stronger urge to see the mountain in all its glory, but... for now, the glory of Elysium's enough!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Say, I know the son of Nyx and incarnate of Death to whom that rather sinister scythe belongs. As do you, in a way! I could give it back to him, rather than getting sliced?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for the generous offer, but I have need of it right now. Besides, Nyx knows where to find me if her son ever needs it back. How are you and Thanatos doing lately anyhow?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Oh we're good. Much better now that we're not frozen in time, same for Megaera. They miss you! She says hello, while he says tell the Princess that she has my gratitude.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Tell him I'm grateful for the scythe!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Well this is awkward. Melinoë, what are you doing here? I was just basking in the adulation of the crowd. They never tire of seeing the Champion or Champions of Elysium fall.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Erm, hello Zagreus! Wow, this is Elysium, but I don't know what I'm doing here. Last I recall I'd picked up some sort of burning parchment from Charon's shop...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Oh gods... you really accepted an Infernal Contract. Read all the fine print, and still thought it was a good idea?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I... can't exactly say I read all the fine print, I mean, there seemed an awful lot, and... could you perhaps summarize what I consented to?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: A free trip to Elysium, for one...! And an exhibition match with its present Champion or Champions for another. Though exhibition does mean deadly battle in this case. You ready?

endLines.speaker: Zagreus
endLines.text: Let's give these Shades a show!

endLines.speaker: Zagreus
endLines.text: Meet the Princess of the Underworld, everyone!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Will I ever see you again beyond the confines of this crowded competitive arena? We ought to be sharing Ambrosia, rather than trying to kill one another for sport.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Nobody knows but the Fates, and from what I've recently been hearing, perhaps not even them! As for the Ambrosia, you've already got a Keepsake from me, so no need!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? Nectar and Ambrosia aren't just for barter! You of everyone must understand the value of forging strong bonds with relatives, comrades, and loved ones.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Of course, but... we've been through quite a bit together at this point! And I've known you since before you were born. But here, we can make it official if you like!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Well all right then! Now to the death?

endLines.speaker: Zagreus
endLines.text: To the death! Or close enough!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Augh... congratulations, Melinoë, for I have deemed you Blasted Strong. Well above Rather Strong on my scale.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Brother, I try. Do you normally even win anything when you prevail in this place?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: ...Usually just Darkness, but... I do have something for you here. I've always wanted to give somebody one of these...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: A Keepsake of some sort? Thank you!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Got you quite decisively last time as I recall, Sister, so I am mildly surprised you'd dare to show your face again in such a competitive environment.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm accustomed to the occasional setback here and there. What about you? You've been trapped in Time. That can't be good for your physical and mental acuity.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I don't know, it's like I didn't miss a beat! Though why don't you be the judge of my physical and mental acuity in just a moment here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're as swift and strong as I've heard, though much more talkative. You're not taking this seriously, are you?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: How dare you, Sister! Although perhaps it's your own seriousness holding you back, you ever think on that?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Augh...! All right, all right, you win this round... and I should go!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Guh...! Well, I think it's time I made my dramatic exit... heh...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Ungh...! Would you... look at the time...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Augh...! I forgot... something I need... back at the House...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Eugh... well go get Chronos then... or Typhon... either one...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Augh...! Enjoy... the many benefits... of membership...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Guh... the Shades... they love... a bit of drama... heh.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Augh, I'm fine, I'm... just a little winded... that's all...!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Augh... I ought to... learn some magick... for myself...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Ow, you are a nightmare, let me tell you... not a witch...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Rngh, I... didn't so much as... put a scratch on you...!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Augh, I must have hit you at some point back there... right?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Augh, how did you... beat me so blasted quick...?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Ow, what happened, you... destroyed me in no time at all...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Augh, all right, all right, you win this round... and I should go!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Guh, well would you look at that... we're out of time!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: All right, Little Sister, let's give these Shades of Elysium a match to remember!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Know that if you do beat me it's because I'm going easy on you, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: As much as I'm glad to see you, this attempt was going so well till you showed up...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: We're much alike in that you, too, seem to enjoy accepting Contracts without reading them.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Your magick against my spear. Father's spear. You know what I mean.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: We'll have to catch up sometime without all the violence, but for now?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I've fought through Elysium way too many times to back down now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Our paths have crossed again, Sister! And our weapons are about to.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Brothers and sisters often fight like this as far as I'm aware.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Don't hold back against me now. The crowd won't have it!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Just be thankful you're not fighting the previous Champion.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Well, Shades? Looks like we have a special exhibition match!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: How do you always show up right in my moment of glory?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Oh hey Melinoë, how you're looking well! Though not for long perhaps, sorry...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: You're worse than fighting Father! Don't tell him I said that.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Bring it on, Sister, I can do this all day. Night? Whenever!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Come show me what the esteemed Schelemeus taught you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Always a welcome break from my routine, Sister! Mostly.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Always a pleasure, Sister! Especially when I don't lose.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Far be it from me to disappoint all these expectant Shades, Sister!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you have way more Boons than I do at the moment...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: So, what awful curses and such you have in store this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Come show me what the esteemed Schelemeus taught you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Always a welcome break from my routine, Sister! Mostly.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: That staff may have some reach, but my spear has this nasty pointy bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Ooh I like those blades of yours! Just let me keep my limbs.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Those torches. Huh. You know I'm flame-resistant, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: You're not the first to cross through here with a gigantic axe, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I don't much care for how that skull of yours is looking at me right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you're using that thing again. Well all right, fire at will.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos is going to want that scythe back at some point!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: How good of you to visit once again, Melinoë! Now, to the death!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: This is, more or less, exactly what I thought having a sibling would be like.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Come to pay me back for last time? Well I accept Obols and that's it.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I already know you don't give up easily, no need to keep this up on my account!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: So this is how Father must have felt driving me back again and again.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: You beat our old decrepit grandfather, surely you can beat me?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: My body and my ego both are well-accustomed to defeat, so come on!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Just be thankful I don't fight as fancy as I used to recently.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I'm used to losing repeatedly but this is getting a bit much.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I'll likely get you back at some point. Perhaps even this time!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Being trapped in a temporal prison for a while has not helped my fighting skills at all...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: ...Oof! You did not hold back at all, there. Blast...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, are you all right? I wasn't certain whether this was meant to be more for show or for real.

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: ...I have to assume you decided for real was the way to go. Anyway, if anybody asks, I went easy on my little sister! Now if you'll excuse me, I, erm, lost a little blood...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: Oof... I didn't... bring you any other prizes... for your victory...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's all right. I still have the Contract as an added benefit. Free goods from Charon's Shop is nothing to scoff at!

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm glad... you feel... that way, I... ought to head on back...

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: There came a point in my Security Specialist career where my victories became too numerous to count! So... thanks for last time, reminding me about defeat!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're very welcome. And likewise have my own defeats become a little less common recently. Though I don't know where that leaves us... somebody always has to lose here?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: The Shades would never tolerate it any other way. Well let's make this a victory-or-defeat to never forget!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Got you again last time, Big Brother! Think you can get me back, or shall the Champion of Elysium title transfer to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: Zagreus
dialogueLines.text: I do think I can get you back, and that title is not up for transfer, no. You should see how much of a pain it was to claim from the last guy!
